**Clinical Reasoning:** The retrieved evidence specifically states that "all infants should have laryngoscopy and bronchoscopy before an open surgical repair of TEF or esophageal atresia" to identify the level of the fistula, assess tracheomalacia, and evaluate laryngeal anatomy. This information directly supports the necessity of preoperative tracheobronchoscopy in planning safe and effective surgical repair. Therefore, it matters clinically.
